summary.intent: Fix review comments on a PR (bennyhodl/fix/exec-node-schema-ghost-param) related to exec node schema ghost param, addressing issues raised by Greptile and ensuring the gateway crate compiles cleanly.
summary.outcome: All Greptile review comments were resolved (reverted dead-code change in close_client, removed unrelated migration, fixed stale node_count in disconnect_all_clients), and rustfmt formatting issues were caught and fixed in state.rs and ws.rs.

repo: Greptile is used as an automated code reviewer on this repo; re-triggering a review takes a few minutes to post.
The project uses a pinned nightly rustfmt and a format-check recipe (Justfile line 14) that enforces formatting as part of local CI.
close_client is never called in the codebase, making any was_node/fetch_sub logic inside it dead code.
node_count is an atomic counter that must be incremented/decremented in ws.rs alongside nodes.register/unregister_by_conn calls to stay in sync.

code.finding: Test imports were reformatted from flat use statements to a grouped use{} block by rustfmt.
fetch_add call on node_count was reformatted to multi-line style by rustfmt.
fetch_sub call on node_count was reformatted to multi-line style by rustfmt; this is also where node_count is decremented on node unregister.

workflow: Greptile review threads must be explicitly resolved; comments in the PR body do not create resolvable threads.
Background compile checks (tokio/cargo build) can run in parallel while addressing review comments.
Local CI runs format-check, biome, i18n, and zizmor checks in parallel; a single formatter failure fails the whole suite.

friction: Greptile re-review took time to post after being triggered, causing uncertainty about whether new comments existed.
Rustfmt formatting issues were only caught after committing and running local checks, requiring an extra fixup commit.
open_items: Greptile new review (triggered by user) had not yet posted at session end — may contain additional findings to address.
The session_tool_mode.sql migration was accidentally bundled and removed; its intended PR/branch was not identified in this session.
